turns.content: yá'át'ééh. I need to figure out a study schedule for something and my brain is not cooperating today
Yá'át'ééh! I can help with that. What do you need to study for, and what's the timeline?
my telehealth certification comes up for renewal on May 5. it's 40 hours of online modules I have to complete before then. and before you ask, no I have not started yet

Okay, so May 5 deadline, 40 hours of modules, and you're starting from zero. That gives you roughly 14 weeks. That's actually very doable if we break it up. What does your typical week look like — when do you realistically have time to sit down with these modules?

weekday evenings are hit or miss. some days I get home and I'm done, you know. just spent. weekends are better but Saturday mornings I'm usually out with the sheep and Sunday is family time. so maybe Saturday afternoon and a couple weekday evenings

That's a realistic starting point. If you can do about 3 hours on Saturday afternoons and squeeze in two weekday evenings at about 1 hour each, that's roughly 5 hours a week. At that pace you'd finish the 40 hours in 8 weeks — done by late March with a full month of buffer before the deadline. Does 5 hours a week feel sustainable or like too much?

5 hours a week is doable if I'm disciplined about it. the problem is I hate staring at a screen that long. I look at screens all day for charting. then I come home and it's more screen. my eyes are not 25 anymore

That's a legitimate issue, not just preference. Screen fatigue is real, especially on top of a full day of clinical documentation. A couple things that might help: break the hour-long evening sessions into two 30-minute blocks with a break in between. And for the Saturday sessions, use the 50-10 rule — 50 minutes of modules, 10 minutes away from the screen. Can you do the modules on a tablet so you're at least not at a desk?

I have a tablet. that's a good idea actually. I could sit in the living room instead of at the kitchen table where I do everything else. change the scenery a little. and the 50-10 thing makes sense. I'll go check on the sheep during my breaks, you know. good excuse

Sheep breaks are the best kind of study breaks. Fresh air, a reason to stand up, and they're not going to quiz you on telehealth protocols. So here's a rough plan: start this coming Saturday, 3 hours in the afternoon with breaks. Pick two weeknight evenings — maybe Tuesday and Thursday so they're spaced out — for 1 hour each. Track your hours so you can see progress. Sound workable?
Tuesday and Thursday works. those are my lighter clinic days usually. and I like having it on a schedule because otherwise I'll just keep saying I'll do it tomorrow. you know how that goes

Exactly — a schedule turns it from something you should do into something you're doing at a set time. If you want, write the hours on a calendar and cross them off as you go. Seeing the progress helps when motivation dips. Is there a particular part of the modules you're dreading most?

the regulatory compliance sections. they're dry as dust. the clinical parts I don't mind because I actually use that every day with my remote patients. the compliance stuff though, you know. it's like reading a legal textbook written by someone who has never met a patient

Front-load the compliance sections then. Do those first while your motivation is still fresh in the early weeks. Save the clinical modules you actually enjoy for later — they'll feel like a reward after getting the dry stuff out of the way. And when you're slogging through compliance, just remind yourself why you're doing it. Those remote patients need you to keep the certification current.

they do. that's the thing. I don't love the modules but the telehealth itself has made a real difference. elders who used to have to drive hours to see me can do a video visit from their daughter's phone. I'm not giving that up because I didn't want to stare at a screen for 40 hours

That's your motivation right there — 40 hours of screen time to keep years of access open for people who need it. You've got this. Start Saturday, compliance modules first, sheep breaks built in. And if you fall behind a week, the buffer gives you room to catch up without panic.
ahéhee'. alright. Saturday it is. I'm writing it on the calendar right now before I talk myself out of it
Good — ink on the calendar makes it real. You've got plenty of time if you stay consistent. Good luck with the modules, and give Susie a pat during your study breaks.
